nameMeanings.name: Buckland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the many places in southern England (including nine in Devon) named Buckland, from Old English bōc ‘book’ + land ‘land’, i.e. land held by right of a written charter, as opposed to folcland, land held by right of custom.

variants.name: Bucklin
meanings: Probably also an Americanized form of Swiss German Büchlin, a very rare variant of Büchli (see Buchli ).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Knight Thomas Buckland was born in 1305, in Nether Wallop, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 daughter with Lady Margeret. He died in 1379, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 74.
